posts.title: Income Tax Calculator 2026: Prepare for Future Tax Planning with RupeeKit

brief.contentTitle: Income Tax Calculator 2026: Prepare for Future Tax Planning with RupeeKit
brief.targetCalculatorName: Income Tax Calculator: Old vs New Regime India (FY 2023-24)
brief.searchIntent: Informational and navigational. Users are seeking to understand what 'income tax calculator 2026' implies, how to estimate potential future tax liabilities in India, and how to use a calculator for forward-looking financial planning.
brief.googleDiscoverAngle: Explain income tax calculator 2026 with a simple India-focused RupeeKit calculator example
brief.audience: Indian salaried individuals, self-employed professionals, and anyone interested in understanding and proactively planning for future income tax implications in India.
brief.uniqueValue: This article will go beyond generic information by providing a practical guide on using RupeeKit's existing Income Tax Calculator for future estimations, including a detailed example table, highlighting common planning mistakes, and incorporating India-specific assumptions and context.

pageSections.title: Introduction: What Does 'Income Tax Calculator 2026' Mean for You?
pageSections.description: Setting the stage for understanding future tax planning and the relevance of a 2026 calculator.

pageSections.title: Decoding '2026': Financial Year vs. Assessment Year in India
pageSections.description: Clarifying the common confusion around financial years (FY 2025-26) and assessment years (AY 2026-27) in the Indian tax context.

pageSections.title: How Income Tax Calculators Work: The Basics
pageSections.description: A brief overview of the principles behind income tax calculation, including income components, deductions, and tax slabs.

pageSections.title: Leveraging RupeeKit's Income Tax Calculator for Future Estimates
pageSections.description: Introducing our existing calculator (FY 2023-24) and explaining how it can be used as a powerful tool for forward-looking tax planning, even before 2026 rules are finalized.

pageSections.title: Step-by-Step Guide: Using RupeeKit's Calculator for Hypothetical 2026 Scenarios
pageSections.description: A practical walkthrough with a concrete, India-focused example showing how to input projected income and deductions to estimate future tax liability.

pageSections.title: Hypothetical Scenario Table: Comparing Tax Outcomes for FY 2025-26
pageSections.description: An illustrative table comparing different income levels and regimes (old vs. new) based on current tax laws, projected for a future year.

pageSections.title: Key Factors That Could Influence Your 2026 Tax Liability
pageSections.description: Discussion on potential changes like Union Budget announcements, inflation, income growth, and evolving tax policies.

pageSections.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid When Estimating Future Taxes
pageSections.description: Highlighting pitfalls such as ignoring inflation, not accounting for changing deductions, or relying solely on outdated information.

pageSections.title: Proactive Tax Planning Tips for Indians
pageSections.description: Actionable advice for individuals to prepare for future tax obligations, focusing on investments, savings, and financial discipline.

pageSections.title: Your Questions Answered: Income Tax Calculator 2026 FAQs
pageSections.description: Addressing common user queries related to future tax planning and calculator usage.

pageSections.title: Conclusion: Empowering Your Future Financial Decisions with RupeeKit
pageSections.description: Summarizing the importance of proactive planning and the utility of tools like RupeeKit's calculator.

calculatorEnhancementIdeas: Add a prominent disclaimer on the calculator page stating that calculations are based on current FY rules and future rules (e.g., for FY 2025-26 / AY 2026-27) may vary significantly.
Introduce an optional 'Future Projection' input field where users can specify an expected annual income growth percentage to see how their tax liability might change over time based on current rules.
Include a tooltip or small info icon next to the calculator title explaining the distinction between Financial Year and Assessment Year, especially concerning the '2026' context.
Develop a 'Compare Years' feature allowing users to see their current year's tax vs. a hypothetical future year's tax (based on current rules and projected income/deductions).
cssAnimationIdeas: Subtle hover effects on calculator input fields and result boxes to indicate interactivity and focus.
A smooth, progressive loading animation for the example tables or charts, revealing data points one by one.
Small, engaging animation (e.g., numbers counting up) when the calculator results are displayed, making the output feel dynamic.
A 'scroll to top' button that subtly appears after scrolling down a certain distance, with a smooth scroll animation.
imageDirections: Utilize high-quality, relevant infographics to visually explain complex tax concepts like tax slabs, deduction categories, and the difference between old and new tax regimes.
Include clear, annotated screenshots of the RupeeKit Income Tax Calculator in action, demonstrating the input process and result interpretation.
Create custom charts or graphs illustrating hypothetical tax savings or comparisons between different planning scenarios.
Ensure all images are India-centric where appropriate (e.g., subtle use of Indian currency symbols, relevant financial documents).
Optimize all images for fast loading speed and responsiveness across various devices, especially mobile.
Provide descriptive alt text for all images to improve accessibility and SEO.
Avoid generic stock photos of smiling people; instead, focus on informative and illustrative visuals. Do not use sensational or misleading imagery to ensure Google Discover safety.
trustAndSafetyNotes: Clearly state that the article and calculator provide estimates based on current tax laws (FY 2023-24) and are for informational purposes only.
Emphasize that future tax laws (for FY 2025-26 / AY 2026-27) may change significantly and these calculations are projections based on current understanding.

Include a strong, explicit disclaimer: 'This content is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice, tax advice, or investment advice. Please consult a qualified financial advisor or tax professional for personalized guidance tailored to your specific situation.'
Avoid making any promises of specific tax savings, guaranteed returns, or financial outcomes.
Maintain a neutral, factual, and educational tone throughout the article.
Ensure all examples and hypothetical scenarios are clearly labeled as such and do not represent actual financial advice.
referencesToCollectManually: Official Income Tax Department of India website (incometax.gov.in) for current tax laws, rules, and notifications.
Union Budget documents for the relevant financial years (e.g., FY 2023-24, and any future budgets that might impact FY 2025-26).
Notifications and circulars issued by the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT).
Reputable Indian financial news publications (e.g., Livemint, The Economic Times, Business Standard) for expert analysis and commentary on tax policy.
Publications or insights from recognized chartered accountants or tax consulting firms in India (for general principles and explanations, not specific advice).

sourceToolSnapshot.name: Income Tax Calculator: Old vs New Regime India (FY 2023-24)
sourceToolSnapshot.targetKeyword: income tax calculator old vs new regime India
sourceToolSnapshot.shortDescription: Compare your income tax liability under the Old vs New Tax Regime in India for FY 2023-24. Use RupeeKit's calculator to find out which regime saves you more tax based on your
sourceToolSnapshot.metaDescription: Compare your income tax liability under the Old vs New Tax Regime in India for FY 2023-24. Use RupeeKit's calculator to find out which regime saves

inputs.label: Gross Annual Income (₹)
inputs.help: Your total income before any deductions.

inputs.label: HRA Received (₹)
inputs.help: House Rent Allowance received from your employer.

inputs.label: Rent Paid Annually (₹)
inputs.help: Total rent paid for accommodation in a financial year.

inputs.label: Home Loan Interest (Section 24b) (₹)
inputs.help: Interest paid on housing loan for self-occupied or let-out property (max ₹2,00,000 for self-occupied).

inputs.label: Deductions under Section 80C (₹)
inputs.help: Investments like PPF, EPF, ELSS, life insurance premiums, etc. (max ₹1,50,000).

inputs.label: Deductions under Section 80D (₹)
inputs.help: Health insurance premiums and preventive health check-ups.

inputs.label: Other Eligible Deductions (Old Regime Only) (₹)
inputs.help: Includes deductions like 80CCD(1B) for NPS (up to ₹50,000), 80G, etc. Applicable only for Old Regime.

inputs.label: Professional Tax Paid (₹)
inputs.help: Professional tax paid (max ₹2,500 per annum). Applicable only for Old Regime.

inputs.label: City Type (HRA Factor)
inputs.help: 50% for Metro cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai), 40% for others.

inputs.label: Standard Deduction (₹)
inputs.help: Standard deduction applicable for salaried individuals (₹50,000 for FY 2023-24).

outputs.label: Taxable Income (Old Regime) (₹)
outputs.formula: grossAnnualIncome - min(standardDeduction, 50000) - min(hraReceived, max(0, rentPaidAnnually - (0.1 * grossAnnualIncome)), (cityRatePercent / 100) * grossAnnualIncome) - min(professionalTaxPaid, 2500) - min(deductions80C, 150000) - deductions80D - min(homeLoanInterest24b, 200000) - otherEligibleDeductionsOldRegime

sourceToolSnapshot.formulaExplanation: This calculator helps you compare your estimated tax liability under the Old and New Tax Regimes for FY 2023-24. For the Old Regime, it deducts the standard deduction (an input, capped at ₹50,000), HRA exemption (calculated as the minimum of actual HRA received, rent paid minus 10% of gross income, or 40/50% of gross income based on your 'City Type' input), professional tax, and other specified deductions like 80C, 80D, 24b, and other eligible deductions from your gross annual income to arrive at the taxable income. For the New Regime, it applies the standard deduction (₹50,000 for FY 2023-24) to your gross income. Please note that the 'Illustrative Tax Payable' for both regimes uses a simplified linear tax rate (10% for Old, 7% for New) for comparison purposes only, as actual tax is calculated based on progressive slab rates and Section 87A rebate, which are handled by the frontend component. The calculator then shows the potential illustrative tax savings or loss when opting for the New Regime over the Old.
sourceToolSnapshot.example: Consider Ms. Priya, a salaried individual with an annual gross income of ₹15,00,000. She receives ₹2,00,000 as HRA, pays ₹1,80,000 in annual rent, has ₹1,50,000 in 80C deductions, ₹25,000 in 80D deductions, and pays ₹1,00,000 as home loan interest (Section 24b). She lives in a metro city (HRA factor 50%). The calculator will first determine her HRA exemption, then calculate her taxable income under both regimes by applying relevant deductions and the standard deduction. Finally, it will show her illustrative tax payable for each regime and the potential savings by choosing the optimal one.

faqs.question: What are the main differences between the old and new income tax regimes in India?
faqs.answer: The old regime allows taxpayers to claim various deductions and exemptions (like HRA, LTA, 80C, 80D, etc.) to reduce their taxable income, but has higher tax slab rates. The new regime offers lower tax slab rates but significantly fewer deductions and exemptions, aiming for simplicity. From FY 2023-24, a standard deduction of ₹50,000 is available in both regimes.

faqs.question: Which tax regime is better for me if I have many deductions?
faqs.answer: Generally, if you have significant deductions and exemptions such as HRA, home loan interest, investments under Section 80C (PPF, ELSS), and health insurance premiums under 80D, the Old Tax Regime might result in lower tax liability. Our calculator helps you compare this directly.

faqs.question: Can I switch between the old and new tax regimes every year?
faqs.answer: Salaried individuals without business income can choose between the old and new tax regimes each financial year when filing their Income Tax Return. However, individuals with business income have restrictions on switching back and forth once they opt for the new regime.

faqs.question: What deductions are still allowed in the new tax regime?
faqs.answer: From FY 2023-24, the new tax regime allows a standard deduction of ₹50,000 for salaried individuals and pensioners. Beyond this, very few other deductions are permitted, primarily employer's contribution to NPS (Section 80CCD(2)) and Agniveer Corpus Fund.

faqs.question: How does HRA exemption impact my tax liability under the old vs new regime?
faqs.answer: HRA exemption is a significant benefit available only under the Old Tax Regime. It reduces your taxable income, potentially leading to lower tax. In the New Tax Regime, HRA exemption is not available, which means your gross income is taxed without this reduction, except for the standard deduction.

faqs.question: Is this calculator financial or tax advice?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is for educational and informational purposes only. It provides an estimate based on the values entered and should not be treated as tax, legal, investment, or financial advice.

posts.codeAssistPrompt:
# Gemini Code Assist Prompt — RupeeKit Post 1

You are editing the RupeeKit Next.js project. Do not publish directly without review. Create or improve a calculator-backed content page using the details below.

## Target

- Site: https://www.rupeekit.co.in
- Content title: Income Tax Calculator 2026: Prepare for Future Tax Planning with RupeeKit
- Suggested slug: `income-tax-calculator-2026-calculator-guide`
- Target calculator: `income-tax-calculator-old-vs-new-regime-india` — Income Tax Calculator: Old vs New Regime India (FY 2023-24)
- Search intent: Informational and navigational. Users are seeking to understand what 'income tax calculator 2026' implies, how to estimate potential future tax liabilities in India, and how to use a calculator for forward-looking financial planning.
- Google Discover angle: Explain income tax calculator 2026 with a simple India-focused RupeeKit calculator example

## Content requirements

Write human-readable, India-focused content. Avoid generic AI filler. Every section should either explain a calculation, show an example, answer a real user question, or help the user use the calculator correctly.

### Required page sections
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]

### Primary keywords
- income tax calculator 2026
- income tax India 2026
- future tax planning India
- income tax estimation India

### Secondary keywords
- new tax regime 2026
- old tax regime 2026
- income tax slabs 2026 India
- tax planning tips India
- financial year 2025-26 tax
- assessment year 2026-27 tax
- RupeeKit income tax calculator
- income tax budget changes India

### Calculator improvements
- Add a prominent disclaimer on the calculator page stating that calculations are based on current FY rules and future rules (e.g., for FY 2025-26 / AY 2026-27) may vary significantly.
- Introduce an optional 'Future Projection' input field where users can specify an expected annual income growth percentage to see how their tax liability might change over time based on current rules.
- Include a tooltip or small info icon next to the calculator title explaining the distinction between Financial Year and Assessment Year, especially concerning the '2026' context.
- Develop a 'Compare Years' feature allowing users to see their current year's tax vs. a hypothetical future year's tax (based on current rules and projected income/deductions).

## Implementation rules

1. Preserve existing calculator formula logic unless a human explicitly asks to change it.
2. Use numeric inputs only for calculator fields.
3. Formula syntax must remain compatible with the current RupeeKit parser.
4. Add clear assumptions, examples, FAQ, and internal links.
5. Add schema suggestions where appropriate: FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, SoftwareApplication/Calculator-like structured data if already supported by the project.
6. Add or confirm `max-image-preview:large` is enabled in site metadata/robots configuration.
7. Do not include financial advice, tax advice, investment recommendations, guaranteed returns, or fake urgency.

## CSS animation requirement

Implement subtle animation only where it improves understanding:
- Subtle hover effects on calculator input fields and result boxes to indicate interactivity and focus.
- A smooth, progressive loading animation for the example tables or charts, revealing data points one by one.
- Small, engaging animation (e.g., numbers counting up) when the calculator results are displayed, making the output feel dynamic.
- A 'scroll to top' button that subtly appears after scrolling down a certain distance, with a smooth scroll animation.

Prefer CSS transitions and lightweight React state. Avoid heavy animation libraries unless already installed. Keep performance mobile-friendly.

## Image handling

Do not call Gemini image API from the website code. The automation will create blank RupeeKit logo templates and prompts. Add image slots/placeholders so the reviewed final image can be placed later.

Website hero prompt to use separately:
Create a clean finance calculator hero image.

## Manual source checklist
- Official Income Tax Department of India website (incometax.gov.in) for current tax laws, rules, and notifications.
- Union Budget documents for the relevant financial years (e.g., FY 2023-24, and any future budgets that might impact FY 2025-26).
- Notifications and circulars issued by the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT).
- Reputable Indian financial news publications (e.g., Livemint, The Economic Times, Business Standard) for expert analysis and commentary on tax policy.
- Publications or insights from recognized chartered accountants or tax consulting firms in India (for general principles and explanations, not specific advice).

## Acceptance criteria

- Page is useful without depending on search traffic.
- Calculator is visible above or near the key explanation.
- Content has a worked example and assumptions.
- Metadata title is under ~60 characters where practical.
- Meta description is under ~155 characters where practical.
- No hallucinated official rules or claims.
- Build/lint passes.

posts.title: SIP Calculator Monthly Investment: Plan Your Wealth Growth in India with RupeeKit

brief.contentTitle: SIP Calculator Monthly Investment: Plan Your Wealth Growth in India with RupeeKit
brief.searchIntent: Informational and navigational. Users are looking to understand how SIP monthly investments work, calculate potential returns, and find a reliable tool to do so.
brief.googleDiscoverAngle: Explain sip calculator monthly investment with a simple India-focused RupeeKit calculator example
brief.audience: New and existing retail investors in India, individuals planning long-term financial goals (e.g., retirement, child's education, down payment), and anyone seeking to understand the mechanics and benefits of Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) through monthly contributions.
brief.uniqueValue: An India-focused, comprehensive guide that integrates RupeeKit's SIP calculator with practical, relatable examples, highlights common investment mistakes specific to the Indian context, and provides clear explanations, moving beyond generic AI-generated content. It will emphasize the 'how-to' of using the calculator for monthly investments.

pageSections.title: Introduction: Unlocking Wealth with Monthly SIPs
pageSections.description: Briefly introduce SIPs, their importance for regular investors in India, and how RupeeKit's SIP calculator helps plan monthly investments.

pageSections.title: What is a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP)?
pageSections.description: Define SIP, explain its core principle of regular, disciplined investing, and highlight the benefits like rupee cost averaging and compounding.

pageSections.title: How RupeeKit's SIP Calculator Monthly Investment Works
pageSections.description: Detailed explanation of the calculator's functionality. Integrate the 'SIP Calculator India' tool directly here. Explain each input field (monthly investment, tenure, expected return) and how it influences the output. Provide a step-by-step example using the calculator.

pageSections.title: Key Inputs for Your Monthly SIP Calculation
pageSections.description: Break down each input: 'Monthly Investment Amount' (how to decide), 'Investment Period' (short vs. long term goals), and 'Expected Annual Return' (realistic expectations, historical data context).

pageSections.title: Understanding Your SIP Calculator Results
pageSections.description: Explain the output fields: 'Total Investment', 'Wealth Gained', and 'Maturity Amount'. Emphasize that these are projections, not guarantees.

pageSections.title: Benefits of Investing Monthly via SIP
pageSections.description: Elaborate on the advantages: discipline, rupee cost averaging, power of compounding, flexibility, and accessibility for small investors.

pageSections.title: Factors Affecting Your SIP Returns
pageSections.description: Discuss market volatility, fund performance, expense ratios, and inflation's impact on real returns. Avoid making predictions.

pageSections.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid with Monthly SIP Investments
pageSections.description: Address pitfalls like stopping SIPs during market downturns, having unrealistic return expectations, not reviewing investments, and investing without clear goals. (Aligns with 'whyRupeeKitCanWin' point).

pageSections.title: Who Should Use a Monthly SIP Calculator?
pageSections.description: Target different user personas and their financial goals (e.g., retirement planning, child's education, buying a home, wealth creation).

pageSections.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) About SIP Monthly Investment
questions: Is monthly SIP investment safe in India?
What is a good monthly SIP amount to start with?
Can I stop or pause my monthly SIP anytime?
How does SIP differ from a lump sum investment?
What is the minimum monthly SIP amount in India?
How often should I review my SIP investments?

pageSections.title: Conclusion: Your Path to Financial Growth Starts Here
pageSections.description: Summarize the benefits of disciplined monthly SIP investing and encourage users to utilize the RupeeKit SIP calculator for their financial planning.

calculatorEnhancementIdeas: **Scenario Comparison Tool:** Allow users to compare 2-3 different SIP scenarios side-by-side (e.g., different monthly amounts, different tenures, or varying expected returns) to visualize impact.
**Goal-Based SIP Planning:** Add an input field where users can specify a financial goal (e.g., '₹X Lakhs in Y years') and the calculator suggests the required monthly SIP amount.
**Inflation-Adjusted Returns:** Provide an option to show the 'real' value of the maturity amount after adjusting for a user-defined or average Indian inflation rate.
**Interactive Growth Chart:** A dynamic line graph showing the growth of total investment vs. wealth gained over the investment period.
**Download/Share Results:** Option to download the calculation results as a PDF or share a direct link to the specific calculation.
cssAnimationIdeas: **Subtle Number Increment:** When calculation results appear, the numbers (total investment, wealth gained, maturity amount) gently animate from zero or a lower value to their final calculated value.
**Progress Bar for Tenure:** A subtle animation on the investment tenure input, perhaps a small bar filling up as the period increases.
**Coin/Rupee Icon Animation:** Small, tasteful animation of a rupee coin or growth arrow appearing or subtly moving near the 'Wealth Gained' section.
**Input Field Focus Effect:** A clean, minimal border or background highlight animation when an input field is focused, guiding the user's attention.
imageDirections: **Discover-Safe & High Quality:** All images must be high-resolution, relevant, and avoid clickbait or overly promotional elements. Focus on clarity and informational value.

**India-Specific Context:** Use images that resonate with an Indian audience. Examples: people (diverse age groups, genders) using laptops/phones to plan finances, Indian currency notes/coins, growth charts with Indian context.
**Visualizing Compounding:** Infographics or charts that visually explain the power of compounding over time with monthly investments.
**Calculator Screenshots:** Clear, well-labeled screenshots of the RupeeKit SIP Calculator in action, demonstrating input fields and results.
**Goal-Oriented Imagery:** Images representing common financial goals in India: a family saving for a child's education, a couple planning for retirement, a person looking at a new home.
trustAndSafetyNotes: **No Financial Advice:** Explicitly state that the content and calculator are for informational and educational purposes only, not financial advice.
**No Guaranteed Returns:** Clearly mention that past performance is not indicative of future results and expected returns are hypothetical projections.
**No Investment Promises:** Avoid language that promises specific returns or outcomes.
**No Tax Advice:** Do not offer tax-related guidance; advise consulting a qualified tax professional.
**Encourage Professional Consultation:** Recommend users consult a SEBI-registered financial advisor before making any investment decisions.
**Avoid Urgency/FOMO:** Do not use language that creates a sense of urgency or fear of missing out (FOMO) on investment opportunities.
referencesToCollectManually: Official SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) guidelines on mutual funds and investment regulations.
AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India) data, reports, and investor awareness materials.
Reputable Indian financial news outlets (e.g., Livemint, Economic Times, Business Standard) for market trends and expert opinions.
RBI (Reserve Bank of India) publications or data relevant to inflation and economic indicators in India.
Academic papers or studies on investment behavior and SIP effectiveness in emerging markets like India (if accessible and relevant).

sourceToolSnapshot.name: SIP Calculator India
sourceToolSnapshot.shortDescription: Estimate future value of monthly SIP investments using expected annual return and investment duration.
sourceToolSnapshot.metaDescription: Free SIP calculator for India. Estimate future value, invested amount and potential gains from monthly SIP investments.

inputs.label: Expected annual return
inputs.help: Expected average annual return. This is not guaranteed.

inputs.label: Investment duration
inputs.help: How long you plan to continue the SIP.

sourceToolSnapshot.formulaExplanation: The calculator uses a monthly compounded future value estimate for SIP investments. Actual returns are market-linked and not guaranteed.
sourceToolSnapshot.example: A ₹10,000 monthly SIP for 10 years at 12% expected annual return gives an estimated future corpus based on monthly compounding assumptions.

faqs.question: Are SIP returns guaranteed?
faqs.answer: No. SIP returns depend on market performance and the selected mutual fund or investment product.

faqs.question: Should I use pre-tax or post-tax returns?
faqs.answer: For realistic planning, consider post-tax and post-expense expectations, especially for long-term goals.

posts.codeAssistPrompt:
# Gemini Code Assist Prompt — RupeeKit Post 2

You are editing the RupeeKit Next.js project. Do not publish directly without review. Create or improve a calculator-backed content page using the details below.

## Target

- Site: https://www.rupeekit.co.in
- Content title: SIP Calculator Monthly Investment: Plan Your Wealth Growth in India with RupeeKit
- Suggested slug: `sip-calculator-monthly-investment-calculator-guide`
- Target calculator: `sip-calculator-india` — SIP Calculator India
- Search intent: Informational and navigational. Users are looking to understand how SIP monthly investments work, calculate potential returns, and find a reliable tool to do so.
- Google Discover angle: Explain sip calculator monthly investment with a simple India-focused RupeeKit calculator example

## Content requirements

Write human-readable, India-focused content. Avoid generic AI filler. Every section should either explain a calculation, show an example, answer a real user question, or help the user use the calculator correctly.

### Required page sections
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]
- [object Object]

### Primary keywords
- sip calculator monthly investment
- sip
- investment
- calculator

### Secondary keywords
- monthly sip calculator
- sip investment plan
- best sip calculator india
- how to calculate sip monthly
- sip return calculator
- sip benefits
- long term sip investment
- rupeekit sip calculator
- sip planning india
- systematic investment plan monthly

### Calculator improvements
- **Scenario Comparison Tool:** Allow users to compare 2-3 different SIP scenarios side-by-side (e.g., different monthly amounts, different tenures, or varying expected returns) to visualize impact.
- **Goal-Based SIP Planning:** Add an input field where users can specify a financial goal (e.g., '₹X Lakhs in Y years') and the calculator suggests the required monthly SIP amount.
- **Inflation-Adjusted Returns:** Provide an option to show the 'real' value of the maturity amount after adjusting for a user-defined or average Indian inflation rate.
- **Interactive Growth Chart:** A dynamic line graph showing the growth of total investment vs. wealth gained over the investment period.
- **Download/Share Results:** Option to download the calculation results as a PDF or share a direct link to the specific calculation.

## Implementation rules

1. Preserve existing calculator formula logic unless a human explicitly asks to change it.
2. Use numeric inputs only for calculator fields.
3. Formula syntax must remain compatible with the current RupeeKit parser.
4. Add clear assumptions, examples, FAQ, and internal links.
5. Add schema suggestions where appropriate: FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, SoftwareApplication/Calculator-like structured data if already supported by the project.
6. Add or confirm `max-image-preview:large` is enabled in site metadata/robots configuration.
7. Do not include financial advice, tax advice, investment recommendations, guaranteed returns, or fake urgency.

## CSS animation requirement

Implement subtle animation only where it improves understanding:
- **Subtle Number Increment:** When calculation results appear, the numbers (total investment, wealth gained, maturity amount) gently animate from zero or a lower value to their final calculated value.
- **Progress Bar for Tenure:** A subtle animation on the investment tenure input, perhaps a small bar filling up as the period increases.
- **Coin/Rupee Icon Animation:** Small, tasteful animation of a rupee coin or growth arrow appearing or subtly moving near the 'Wealth Gained' section.
- **Input Field Focus Effect:** A clean, minimal border or background highlight animation when an input field is focused, guiding the user's attention.

Prefer CSS transitions and lightweight React state. Avoid heavy animation libraries unless already installed. Keep performance mobile-friendly.

## Image handling

Do not call Gemini image API from the website code. The automation will create blank RupeeKit logo templates and prompts. Add image slots/placeholders so the reviewed final image can be placed later.

Website hero prompt to use separately:
Create a clean finance calculator hero image.

## Manual source checklist
- Official SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) guidelines on mutual funds and investment regulations.
- AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India) data, reports, and investor awareness materials.
- Reputable Indian financial news outlets (e.g., Livemint, Economic Times, Business Standard) for market trends and expert opinions.
- RBI (Reserve Bank of India) publications or data relevant to inflation and economic indicators in India.
- Academic papers or studies on investment behavior and SIP effectiveness in emerging markets like India (if accessible and relevant).

## Acceptance criteria

- Page is useful without depending on search traffic.
- Calculator is visible above or near the key explanation.
- Content has a worked example and assumptions.
- Metadata title is under ~60 characters where practical.
- Meta description is under ~155 characters where practical.
- No hallucinated official rules or claims.
- Build/lint passes.
